tasks.title: Native per-resource resolver core: Target -> CredentialBundle entry point, config-not-code (TG-89)

scenarios: A target resolves to exactly one credential bundle from config data
A credential bundle is constructible only with all required fields

tasks.title: Fail-closed resolver: unresolved -> refuse, no default-identity fallback (TG-89)

scenarios: An unresolved target is refused with no default identity
The fail-closed property holds under an empty or partially-synced store

tasks.title: Sealed-at-rest SecretRef binding: env/file/store schemes, no plaintext credential (TG-89)

scenarios: Every credential value is a SecretRef resolved at runtime and no plaintext is stored

tasks.title: Shared estate object-model match + most-specific-wins precedence (TG-89)

scenarios: The resolver matches on the same estate object-model as the policy engine
A multi-rule target resolves by most-specific-wins and an equal-specificity conflict refuses

tasks.title: Compose after policy authZ, before execute: authN as a distinct control layer (TG-98)

scenarios: Identity is resolved after a non-deny policy verdict and before execute

tasks.title: Two-plane router: machine->host vs human->console approver, never crossed (TG-108)

scenarios: A machine-plane source cannot populate approver identity
An approver-plane source cannot populate a host credential bundle

tasks.title: Unified CredentialSource interface + scheduled/on-demand Sync, incremental+idempotent, cross-source precedence, last-synced+drift (TG-108)

scenarios: A source syncs read-only into the native store on schedule and on demand
A repeated sync of unchanged data converges with no duplicate or orphan
A target in multiple sources resolves by source precedence and records shadowed sources
A target with no synced source resolves from the native-store fallback
Each source records last-synced and drift

tasks.title: AWX/Ansible/Semaphore connector: read-only inventory + credential pull, native Go, distroless (TG-88)

scenarios: The AWX Ansible Semaphore source pulls inventory and credentials read-only with no subprocess

tasks.title: OpenBao/HashiCorp Vault connector: vault:/bao: store resolver, KV v2, fail-closed on unreachable/denied/expired (TG-90)

scenarios: The vault bao SecretRef scheme reads KV v2 read-only and fails closed on unreachable or denied or expired

tasks.title: LDAP/OIDC connector: human-plane users+groups feeding spec/015 approve_by (TG-108)

scenarios: The LDAP OIDC source pulls approver identities read-only and never writes a host bundle

tasks.title: Secondary governed Ansible sensor + actuator behind the mode chokepoint and never-auto floor (TG-88)

scenarios: Read-only fact-gathering is a Phase-1-safe sensor and playbook actuation routes through the mode chokepoint

tasks.title: credential_resolution + credential_sync_run persistence migration, audit-every-resolution, registered-only sources (TG-107)

scenarios: Every resolution and sync is appended to the governance ledger with no secret value
The credential tables are append-only with no runtime UPDATE or DELETE
A source is admitted only when its connector adapter is registered at startup

tasks.title: First-class Phase-1 console credential UX: sync-source config/test/schedule/Sync-now, per-target credential map, coverage view paired with policy packet-tracer, shared object-groups editor, sealed write-only ledger-audited edits (TG-109)

scenarios: The console renders per-source last-synced and drift
The console configures tests schedules and syncs each source on demand
The console renders the per-target credential map with source and precedence
The console coverage view answers whether TG can reach a target
The console object-groups editor is shared with the policy engine
A secret value is write-only in the console and never echoed

tasks.title: OIDC client-credentials token minter: oidc: SecretRef scheme, machine-plane Bearer token, verified TLS, cached by expires_in, fail-closed (TG-107)

scenarios: The oidc SecretRef scheme mints a machine-plane client-credentials token and fails closed on unreachable or denied or untrusted-TLS
